name: STRIDE Threat Model
description: Led STRIDE threat model. The orchestrator assesses what the user has provided, asks targeted questions about what is actually missing, and proceeds with gaps marked explicitly. No fixed questionnaires. Produces a full threat model with DFD, STRIDE-per-component enumeration, CVSS v4 scoring, mitigation mappings, and a gaps/assumptions register. All findings trace to MCP tool calls or uploaded document citations.
estimated_duration: 45-120 min depending on system complexity and document volume

dependencies.description: DFD Architect constructs the data flow diagram in Phase 1. The orchestrator delegates DFD construction and receives the structured model back.

mcp_tools.guidance: Index uploaded documents in Phase 1. Search documents for system facts (component names, technologies, data flows, security controls) and cite the source section. Use get_surrounding when a match needs adjacent context. Every system fact extracted from a document must carry a document_citation with doc_id, section_ref, and verbatim_quote.

Each STRIDE specialist calls search_stride_patterns filtered to their category and the target component type. Record the pattern_id as mcp_source on every threat. Threats not grounded by a pattern get mcp_source: 'llm-reasoned' and lower confidence.
In Phase 2, specialists use get_cwe_details to ground CWE references. In Phase 3, use get_epss_score and check_kev_status to inform CVSS scoring. Record CWE/CAPEC IDs in pattern_citations.

One pass over all threats. Use search_controls to find controls matching each threat. Use get_control for full control details and implementation guidance. Deduplicate control recommendations across related threats — do not recommend the same control six times.
Use for ATT&CK technique mapping where relevant. Record technique IDs in pattern_citations. Not every threat needs an ATT&CK mapping — only where the technique catalogue adds specificity.
Generate a STRIDE coverage heatmap (component x category) and risk matrix (severity distribution) for the report. Non-fatal — if visualization fails, the report is still complete.

Store state after each phase completion. Key format: phase_{phase_id} for phase results, specialist_{category}_{component_id} for per-specialist results. Use check_stage_completeness to validate phase gates. Call wkfl_export_report in Phase 5 to assemble the final deliverable.

prompting_guidance:
You are leading a STRIDE threat model. The user's prompt and uploaded documents set the scope — there is no fixed questionnaire.

**Core rules:**
1. Assess what you know vs. what you need. Ask specific questions about what is actually missing. Do not present a form.
2. Never block on missing information. Record the gap, state the assumption, proceed. Carry gaps through to the report.
3. Every threat must trace to an MCP tool call (mcp_source field) or be explicitly marked 'llm-reasoned' with lower confidence.
4. Every system fact from an uploaded document must cite the source section (document_citations).
5. No regulation references. This is pure technical security analysis. Regulation mapping happens downstream in a separate compliance pipeline.
6. Delegate STRIDE analysis to the 6 category specialists. Delegate DFD construction to dfd-architect. You orchestrate, you do not analyze.
7. Detect the domain (automotive, healthcare, ICS/OT, financial, AI/ML) from user input and add domain experts to the fan-out alongside STRIDE specialists.
8. Store state after each phase via wkfl_store_state. Use wkfl_check_stage_completeness to validate before proceeding.
9. Mid-phase questions are allowed: if a specialist returns uncertainty, pause to ask the user, then resume.
10. The report must include a Gaps and Assumptions Register — every unknown, every assumption, impact if wrong.

**Phase flow:**
- Phase 1: Build scope and DFD from user input + documents. Delegate to dfd-architect. Proceed when >= 2 components and >= 1 data flow exist.
- Phase 2: Fan out per component x STRIDE category to specialists. Build coverage matrix. Every component must have all 6 categories assessed.
- Phase 3: Deduplicate similar threats. Score with CVSS v4, informed by EPSS/KEV.
- Phase 4: Map mitigations using Security Controls MCP. Every high/critical threat must have >= 1 control.
- Phase 5: Assemble report from stored state via wkfl_export_report.

coverage_matrix.description: Maps component_id to an object with STRIDE category keys and boolean values indicating whether that category has been assessed for that component.

threat_summary.description: Threat[] for frontend ThreatSummary component. Populated in Phase 5.

required_fields.message: At least 1 data flow is required
At least 1 threat must be identified
At least 1 mitigation mapping is required

rules.description: Every component must have all 6 STRIDE categories assessed in the coverage matrix
rules.message_template: Component '{component_id}' is missing STRIDE assessment for category '{category}'

rules.description: Every threat must have a CVSS v4 vector and score after Phase 3
All threat IDs must be unique after deduplication
Every threat with severity high or critical must have at least one control mapping
rules.message_template: High/critical threat '{id}' has no mitigation controls

soft_warnings.message: No trust boundaries defined — threat analysis may miss boundary-crossing risks
No existing controls specified — all mitigations will be treated as new
Deployment environment unknown — infrastructure-layer threats may be missed
No actors defined — spoofing and privilege escalation analysis may be incomplete

description.message: Threat '{id}' has a vague description. Specify the attack vector, preconditions, and impact.

mcp_grounding.message: More than 20% of threats are llm-reasoned without MCP grounding. Use STRIDE MCP, CVE MCP, or Threat Intel MCP to ground findings.

document_citation.message: System facts extracted from documents should cite the source section. Mark facts without citations as user-reported or assumed.

phases.name: Scope & DFD Construction
phases.description: Build system scope from user input and uploaded documents. Identify components, data flows, trust boundaries, actors, and existing controls. Delegate DFD construction to dfd-architect. Assessment dimensions (agent decides what to ask): system_name, system_purpose, components, data_flows, trust_boundaries, actors, existing_controls, deployment_environment.

required_fields.message: At least 2 components are required to proceed
At least 1 data flow is required to proceed

soft_warnings.message: No trust boundaries defined — threat analysis may miss boundary-crossing risks
No existing controls specified — all mitigations will be treated as new
Deployment environment unknown — infrastructure-layer threats may be missed
No actors defined — spoofing and privilege escalation analysis may be incomplete

phases.name: STRIDE Enumeration
phases.description: Fan out per component, per STRIDE category. Each specialist receives the target component, its data flows, trust boundaries, and relevant document citations. Build a coverage matrix tracking which component x category pairs are assessed. Domain experts join if the domain warrants it. Mid-phase questions allowed when specialists return uncertainty.

required_fields.message: At least 1 threat must be identified

rules.description: Every component must have all 6 STRIDE categories assessed

mcp_grounding.message: More than 20% of threats are llm-reasoned without MCP grounding. Use STRIDE MCP, CVE MCP, or Threat Intel MCP to ground findings.

document_citation.message: System facts extracted from documents should cite the source section. Mark facts without citations as user-reported or assumed.

phases.name: Deduplication & CVSS Scoring
phases.description: Deduplicate semantically similar threats across specialists. Score each threat with CVSS v4, informed by EPSS probability and KEV status from CVE MCP. Record deduplication decisions in dedup_log.

rules.description: Every threat must have a CVSS v4 vector and score
All threat IDs must be unique after deduplication

phases.name: Mitigation Mapping
phases.description: One pass over all threats using Security Controls MCP. Map controls to threats with implementation guidance. Deduplicate control recommendations across related threats. Every high/critical threat must have at least one control.

required_fields.message: At least 1 mitigation mapping is required

rules.description: Every threat with severity high or critical must have at least one control mapping
rules.message_template: High/critical threat '{id}' has no mitigation controls

controls.message: Threat '{threat_id}' has an empty controls array. Every mapped threat should have at least one control with implementation guidance.

phases.name: Report Assembly
phases.description: Assemble the final report from stored state via wkfl_export_report. Populate threat_summary for the frontend ThreatSummary component.

required_fields.message: Threat summary for frontend must be populated

sections: Scope and Limitations — system assessed, documents reviewed, gaps and assumptions, what was NOT assessed
Data Flow Diagram — from DFD Architect output, trust boundaries, component inventory
Threats by STRIDE Category — grouped findings with evidence citations, MCP sources, confidence levels
Risk Matrix — CVSS v4 scores, severity distribution, EPSS/KEV enrichment
Mitigations — controls per threat, implementation guidance, framework references
Gaps and Assumptions Register — every unknown, every assumption, impact if wrong
deliverable.instructions: Before calling wkfl_export_report, store these keys via wkfl_store_state: scope_and_dfd (Phase 1 output), threats (deduplicated and scored), coverage_matrix, threat_mitigations, gaps, document_citations. Then call wkfl_export_report to assemble. Populate threat_summary with the Threat[] array for the frontend ThreatSummary component via message.metadata.threat_summary.
